# Transcrição: Adapting_to_others_better

## [00:00 - 01:19]

The biggest mistake people make is they think, if I'm going to be authentic, I'm always just going to be me, irregardless of who you are. Look, that's true to a certain extent. Like nothing is 100% blanket statements. But what I believe is you learn these foundations so that you have gears now. So if I'm not in teacher mode, you'll find that I'm slightly different as well, because I'll adapt to you. Because imagine I'm at a client and a lot of CEOs are fairly, a lot of them in the US are quite introverts. So they go, oh, haven't, thanks for coming to my conference and speaking at the conference. So if I came in and I went, oh, great to see you, John. Yeah, mate, so excited. Boom, whoa, there's too much. So I kind of come in and go, oh yeah, hey, no worries. Love to be a thanks for having me. Oh my God. So I still have enthusiasm, but it's less volume because his volume is so low. So I bring it down. So you're looking at the way people are using their foundations, you meet them where they are and you slowly try to bring them out. So you slowly increase your volume and you go, oh, thanks John, I appreciate it. But hey, John, love the food. And then you just kind of see them go, oh yeah, we got that because, and you watch their kind of volume increase as well. And it's not that you're changing your values and who you are, you're just playing your instrument to harmonize. Key word, harmonize. You're harmonizing your instrument with theirs. And the moment you harmonize your instrument with theirs, they feel a sense of connection.
